item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for drawing that to my attention - seems I forgot to upload the file directly to this page and it was still being hosted on the sandbox, which I recently cleaned out. Oops! For those reading this - always keep backups!

Anyways, since the original was wiped and I have no screenshots of it, had to recreate the image from scratch - hope this one suffices!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the photo supposed to be like that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, dammit. I must’ve missed that sentence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah when I first read about the reparations I was like "since when does the Foundation have the budget for that?" but then I got to the last message and thought "wow, that's actually pretty neat."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, it's the extradimensional entities that set up the accounts that the foundation is using. They're taking responsibility by paying out of pocket, which is something you don't see often with SCPs.

Minor quibble: the last email has the foundation email address in both fields.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don’t see why the Foundation should feel inclined to obey some extradimensional society’s legal code, especially when it’s going to run them over 3 billion USD on a single SCP. Otherwise, good article.

item.title: Author's Post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Tanhony & Cregan_Stark for looking at this in IRC Chat!

Fun facts related to the article, for the curious:
– hide block

From UpToDate (essentially Wikipedia for healthcare professionals):
On comas and vegetative states: "Coma is defined as a state of pathologic unconsciousness; patients are unaware of their environment and are unarousable… Coma must be distinguished from the persistent vegetative state, which is also characterized by unawareness, but in which patients have normal sleep-wake cycles and are arousable. Patients in a coma may progress to a vegetative state, but this may not be associated with an improvement in their overall functional outcome… Both coma and persistent vegetative states must be distinguished from brain death…"

On brain death: "Brain death (death by brain criteria) is defined as the irreversible cessation of cerebral and brainstem function. There is no respiratory drive, and thus there are no spontaneous breaths regardless of hypercarbia or hypoxemia. There are no responses arising from the brain (including cranial nerve reflexes and motor responses) to stimuli, although spinal reflexes may persist"

Where did I get the Qatari lifetime value number?
"No One Values Your Life More than the US Government." https://www.bloomberg.com/graphics/2017-value-of-life/. Took the math from there, multiplied it by two to reflect the fact that Qatar has nearly double the GNI per capita of the United States (based off https://www.usatoday.com/story/money/2019/07/07/richest-countries-in-the-world/39630693/).

Edit: Regarding the image, I made it myself via Microsoft Paint. I guess I declare that this is under a Creative Commons license as I am the owner?
¯\_(ツ)_/¯
